Benefit for Child Identity: Teaches discipline and starting the day with God's remembrance
Parental Tip: Wake them up gently and encourage using a prayer diary to record feelings.

Time Range: From noon until the shadow is equal to the object
Benefit for Child Identity: Provides a spiritual break during the busy day to reconnect
Parental Tip: Model good behavior by pausing your own work to pray.

Time Range: From the end of Dhuhr until the sun turns yellow/sets
Benefit for Child Identity: Reinforces consistency and responsibility during playtime
Parental Tip: Discuss which prayer is most difficult for them and why during weekly reviews.

Time Range: From sunset until the red twilight disappears
Ruling/Requirement: Mandatory (Fard); First two rakats are loud
Benefit for Child Identity: Strengthens family bonds through congregational evening prayer
Parental Tip: Use the prayer diary to track commitment and reward consistency.

Time Range: From the end of Maghrib until midnight
Ruling/Requirement: Mandatory (Fard); First two rakats are loud
Benefit for Child Identity: Ends the day with reflection and psychological peace
Parental Tip: Balance motivation and supervision; be a source of good values before bed.
